name: Bonansa Loop from El Pont de Suert
name_original: Ida y vuelta a Bonansa - Circular desde Pont de Suert
description: Challenge yourself on the Bonansa Loop from El Pont de Suert, a difficult 37.4-mile road cycling route with 5203 feet of elevation gain.
region_intro: Road cycling routes around El Pont De Suert are situated in the Alta Ribagorça region of the Catalan Pyrenees, offering diverse landscapes for cyclists. The area is characterized by the Noguera Ribagorçana valley, flanked by the axial Pyrenees and interior mountain ranges like Serra Moixera and Serra de Sant Martí. This terrain provides a mix of challenging climbs and scenic routes, with well-paved surfaces suitable for road cyclists. Peaks in the vicinity can exceed 2,500 meters, contributing to significant elevation gains on many routes. Best road cycling routes around El Pont De Suert The most popular road cycling route is Bonansa Loop from El Pont de Suert, a 37.4 miles (60.1 km) trail that takes 3 hours 46 minutes to complete. This difficult route features significant elevation gain and panoramic mountain views. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is Valle de Boi - Round Trip through Cavallers & Taull & Boi-Taull, a difficult 50.1 miles (80.6 km) path. This route includes substantial ascent through varied Pyrenean landscapes, passing through the UNESCO World Heritage site of Vall de Boí. Local road cyclists also love the Ermita de Sant Salvador d'Igor - Circular from Pont de Suert, a 15.8 miles (25.4 km) trail leading through the local area with notable elevation gain, often completed in about 1 hour 49 minutes. Road cycling around El Pont De Suert is defined by Pyrenean mountain ranges, river valleys, and protected natural spaces. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate rides to advanced road bike climbs. The routes in El Pont De Suert are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.9 stars from over 19 reviews. More than 1000 road cyclists have used komoot to explore El Pont De Suert's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many road cycling routes are available around El Pont de Suert?
A: There are over 15 dedicated road cycling routes around El Pont de Suert, offering a variety of experiences from moderate rides to challenging climbs. The region's diverse terrain in the Catalan Pyrenees ensures options for different skill levels.

Q: Are there any easy or moderate road cycling routes suitable for beginners or families?
A: While many routes in the region feature significant elevation, there are moderate options available. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e943786618/ermita-de-sant-salvador-d-igor-circular-from-pont-de-suert" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ermita de Sant Salvador d'Igor - Circular from Pont de Suert&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 25.4 km trail that offers a good introduction to the local area without extreme difficulty. Another moderate option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40015852" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Llesp – Sant Feliu de Barruera Church loop from el Pont de Suert&lt;/a&gt;, which is 39.6 km long.

Q: What are some of the most challenging road cycling climbs in the area?
A: El Pont de Suert is known for its demanding climbs. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e944668473/bonansa-loop-from-el-pont-de-suert" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Bonansa Loop from El Pont de Suert&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult 60.1 km route with over 1,500 meters of elevation gain, offering panoramic mountain views. Another significant challenge is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e944605066/valle-de-boi-round-trip-through-cavallers-taull-boi-taull" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Valle de Boi - Round Trip through Cavallers & Taull & Boi-Taull&lt;/a&gt;, an 80.6 km journey with over 2,200 meters of ascent through varied Pyrenean landscapes.

Q: Are there circular road cycling routes available?
A: Yes, many of the road cycling routes around El Pont de Suert are designed as loops. Examples include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e944668473/bonansa-loop-from-el-pont-de-suert" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Bonansa Loop from El Pont de Suert&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e943786618/ermita-de-sant-salvador-d-igor-circular-from-pont-de-suert" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ermita de Sant Salvador d'Igor - Circular from Pont de Suert&lt;/a&gt;, allowing cyclists to start and finish in the same location.

Q: What kind of landscapes and scenic viewpoints can I expect on these routes?
A: Road cycling routes in El Pont de Suert traverse dramatic Pyrenean scenery, including the Noguera Ribagorçana valley, axial Pyrenees, and interior mountain ranges. You'll encounter long, open plains, high-altitude climbs, and stunning vistas. Iconic passes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/269310" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Bonansa Pass&lt;/a&gt; offer demanding ascents rewarded with panoramic mountain views.

Q: What cultural or historical attractions can I visit along the road cycling routes?
A: The region is rich in cultural heritage. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e944605066/valle-de-boi-round-trip-through-cavallers-taull-boi-taull" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Valle de Boi - Round Trip through Cavallers & Taull & Boi-Taull&lt;/a&gt; route passes through the UNESCO World Heritage site of Vall de Boí, where you can visit exceptional Romanesque churches like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3081472" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sant Climent de Taüll&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3081463" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Church of the Nativity in Durro&lt;/a&gt;. The town of El Pont de Suert itself has a well-preserved historic center with notable architectural features.

Q: Are there any natural protected areas or unique natural features to see?
A: Yes, the area includes protected natural spaces such as the Vall Alta de Serradell – Serra de Sant Gervàs, known for its diverse flora and fauna. Other protected areas include La Faiada de Malpàs i Cambatiri and the Salencar de Llesp. You might also encounter features like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2472984" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Lake Montcortés&lt;/a&gt;, offering serene natural beauty.

Q: What do other road cyclists enjoy most about road cycling in El Pont de Suert?
A: The road cycling routes around El Pont de Suert are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.9 stars from over 19 reviews. Cyclists often praise the challenging climbs, the stunning Pyrenean landscapes, and the well-paved surfaces that make for an excellent road biking experience.

Q: When is the best time of year for road biking in El Pont de Suert?
A: The region's Pyrenean location suggests that late spring, summer, and early autumn are generally the most favorable times for road cycling. During these months, the weather is typically more stable, and the higher mountain passes are clear of snow, offering optimal conditions for exploring the diverse terrain.

Q: Is parking available near the road cycling routes in El Pont de Suert?
A: El Pont de Suert serves as a convenient base for cyclists, and parking is generally available within the town. Many routes start directly from the town center or nearby, making it easy to access the trails after parking your vehicle.

Q: Are there any mountain passes that are particularly popular with road cyclists?
A: Absolutely. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/269310" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Bonansa Pass&lt;/a&gt;, located at an altitude of 1,380 meters, is a well-known and demanding climb that is popular among road cyclists for its challenge and the rewarding panoramic views from the top. Other notable passes include &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5288229" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Coll d'Oli&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4998530" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Coll de l'Espina (1407 m)&lt;/a&gt;.
tips: Lookout located at the pass of the town of Montanuy, forming the border between the Catalan and Aragonese provinces.
Hill between valleys not so much a true port. It is a road between hills and some stretch of forest. The rise is smooth and sustained, not exceeding 8%.

The Bonansa pass is a pass that connects the Noguera Ribagorzana river basin, on the border between Aragon and Catalonia, with the Sis valley and the Isábena basin. It is located in the province of Huesca. Long port with good views.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on the Bonansa Loop from El Pont de Suert?
faqs.answer: This route is primarily a challenging road bike ride. You can expect mostly firm paved tracks, suitable for road bikes. However, be prepared for significant elevation gains and steep gradients, as it's classified as a difficult road route requiring very good fitness.

faqs.question: What are the main points of interest along this route?
faqs.answer: The loop offers several notable spots. You'll pass by the Montanuy Viewpoint, offering great vistas. Additionally, you'll traverse mountain passes like Coll de l'Espina (1407 m) and Puerto de Bonansa - west side. The region itself, Alta Ribagorça, is rich in Romanesque heritage and stunning natural landscapes.

faqs.question: Is the Bonansa Loop from El Pont de Suert suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: No, this route is explicitly classified as a "difficult" road bike ride, requiring "very good fitness." It features significant elevation gains and steep gradients, making it unsuitable for beginners or those with limited cycling experience.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to cycle this route?
faqs.answer: Given the mountainous terrain and the nature of road cycling, the best time to cycle this loop is typically during the warmer, drier months, from late spring through early autumn. This avoids snow and ice on the passes and provides more stable weather conditions for a demanding ride.

faqs.question: Where can I park when starting this loop from El Pont de Suert?
faqs.answer: El Pont de Suert serves as the starting point for this loop. While specific parking areas aren't detailed, as a local hub, you should find public parking options within the town to begin your ride. Look for designated parking zones or street parking suitable for a full day.

faqs.question: Are there any permits or fees required to cycle this route?
faqs.answer: No, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required to cycle the Bonansa Loop from El Pont de Suert. It's a public road cycling route through the region.

faqs.question: Is the Bonansa Loop from El Pont de Suert dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: As a road cycling route, the concept of "dog-friendly" is less applicable than for hiking trails. While you might encounter dogs in towns, bringing a dog along for a demanding 60 km road bike ride with significant elevation is generally not practical or safe for the animal.

faqs.question: Does this route intersect with other well-known cycling or hiking paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route intersects with or runs concurrently with sections of several other paths. Notably, it covers parts of the "La Ribagorza. BTT. Ruta 19. Transpirenaica de Bonansa a Castejon de Sos" (25%), "Passo de Espés" (21%), and "Aran Pirineos. Camino de Santiago" (20%). You'll also find smaller segments overlapping with routes like "Ruta Transpirenaica en BTT" and "La Ribagorza. BTT. Ruta 18. Montanuy".

faqs.question: What kind of scenery can I expect along the Bonansa Loop?
faqs.answer: The loop traverses the Alta Ribagorça region, which is known for its stunning natural landscapes and rich cultural heritage. You'll experience picturesque views, especially from viewpoints and mountain passes, and pass through an area recognized for its Romanesque architecture and as a Starlight Reserve, offering clear night skies if you're in the area after dark.

faqs.question: Are there places to stop for food or drink along the route?
faqs.answer: While the route passes through some villages, specific cafes or pubs directly on the route are not detailed. It's always advisable to carry sufficient water and snacks, especially given the route's difficulty and length. You may find options in larger towns like El Pont de Suert at the start/end, or in Bonansa itself.

faqs.question: What should I bring for a challenging road bike ride like this?
faqs.answer: For this difficult road bike loop, you should bring appropriate gear for a long and demanding ride. This includes a well-maintained road bike, helmet, cycling apparel, plenty of water and nutrition, a repair kit (spare tube, pump, tools), navigation device, and layers of clothing suitable for changing mountain weather. Sun protection is also essential.
